---
title: Munich Tourism
---

Munich is one of Germany's most-visited cities. The
[Monatszahlen Tourismus](https://opendata.muenchen.de/dataset/monatszahlen-tourismus)
dataset, published monthly by the Statistisches Amt München, records hotel
guests and overnight stays back to 1992.

The dataset is a single full-history CSV replaced each month on the portal, so
the download URL changes with every update. The pipeline resolves the current
URL via the Munich Open Data CKAN API, then downloads, validates with
[`@schema`](../reference/schema.schema.qmd), and upserts into a Delta Lake
table using [`@incremental`](../reference/incremental.incremental.qmd).

**Data source:** Statistisches Amt München ·
License: [Datenlizenz Deutschland – Namensnennung 2.0](https://www.govdata.de/dl-de/by-2-0)
```{python}
#| echo: false
try:
    from dotenv import load_dotenv
    load_dotenv()
except ImportError:
    print("python-dotenv not found, skipping .env loading") 
```
```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
from pathlib import Path

import altair as alt
import polars as pl

BUCKET = os.environ.get("MUNICH_CYCLING_BUCKET", "data-warehousing-with-polars")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
df = (
    pl.scan_delta(f"s3://{BUCKET}/delta/munich_tourism")
    .select("MONATSZAHL", "AUSPRAEGUNG", "JAHR", "WERT", "date")
    .collect()
    .sort("date")
)
df.head(5)
```

## Monthly overnight stays

Total overnight stays per month since 1992. The COVID-19 collapse in 2020 and
the subsequent recovery stand out sharply — Munich went from a record-breaking
pre-pandemic peak to near-zero almost overnight.

```{python}
#| echo: false
nights = (
    df.filter(
        (pl.col("MONATSZAHL") == "Übernachtungen")
        & (pl.col("AUSPRAEGUNG") == "insgesamt")
    )
    .drop_nulls("WERT")
)

alt.Chart(nights).mark_area(
    line={"color": "#4c78a8"},
    color=alt.Gradient(
        gradient="linear",
        stops=[
            alt.GradientStop(color="rgba(76,120,168,0.6)", offset=0),
            alt.GradientStop(color="rgba(76,120,168,0.05)", offset=1),
        ],
        x1=1, x2=1, y1=1, y2=0,
    ),
).encode(
    x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("WERT:Q", title="Overnight stays", axis=alt.Axis(format="~s")),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("date:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("WERT:Q", title="Overnight stays", format=",.0f"),
    ],
).properties(
    width="container", height=260, title="Munich — total overnight stays per month"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```

## Guests: domestic vs. international

Munich consistently draws more international visitors than domestic tourists —
a pattern the pandemic briefly disrupted as cross-border travel collapsed first.

```{python}
#| echo: false
guests = (
    df.filter(
        (pl.col("MONATSZAHL") == "Gäste")
        & (pl.col("AUSPRAEGUNG").is_in(["Inländer", "Ausländer"]))
    )
    .drop_nulls("WERT")
)

color_scale = alt.Scale(
    domain=["Inländer", "Ausländer"],
    range=["#4c78a8", "#f58518"],
)

selection = alt.selection_point(fields=["AUSPRAEGUNG"], bind="legend")

alt.Chart(guests).mark_line(strokeWidth=1.5).encode(
    x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("WERT:Q", title="Guests", axis=alt.Axis(format="~s")),
    color=alt.Color(
        "AUSPRAEGUNG:N",
        scale=color_scale,
        legend=alt.Legend(title="Origin"),
    ),
    opacity=alt.condition(selection, alt.value(1), alt.value(0.1)),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("date:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("AUSPRAEGUNG:N", title="Origin"),
        alt.Tooltip("WERT:Q", title="Guests", format=",.0f"),
    ],
).add_params(selection).properties(
    width="container", height=260, title="Hotel guests by origin"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```

## Seasonality

Average overnight stays by calendar month, confirming the strong summer and
Oktoberfest-autumn peaks and a trough in January–February.

```{python}
#| echo: false
seasons = (
    nights
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("date").dt.month().alias("month_num"),
        pl.col("date").dt.strftime("%b").alias("month_name"),
    )
    .group_by("month_num", "month_name")
    .agg(pl.col("WERT").mean().alias("avg_nights"))
    .sort("month_num")
)

alt.Chart(seasons).mark_bar(color="#4c78a8").encode(
    x=alt.X("month_name:N", sort=None, title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y(
        "avg_nights:Q",
        title="Avg overnight stays",
        axis=alt.Axis(format="~s"),
    ),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("month_name:N", title="Month"),
        alt.Tooltip("avg_nights:Q", title="Avg stays", format=",.0f"),
    ],
).properties(
    width="container", height=220, title="Average overnight stays by month of year"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```
